name: Sample Player (Scrub)
description: This algorithm is based on the Audio Playback with Scrub algorithm on the disting mk4. In this algorithm the sample playback position is directly driven from the [input]. Imagine a piece of audio tape passing over a tape head – in other algorithms, that tape is constantly moving at some speed or other as if driven by a motor, but in this algorithm you're basically dragging the tape back and forwards over the playhead yourself. You can scrub the audio using a CV input, or the Scrub parameter (which can of course be mapped). Remember that you need to trigger the sample before any playback will occur, and again to change the sample (unless you have ‘Retrigger on change’ on).

specifications.name: Record time
specifications.description: The maximum length of audio that can be recorded (sampled).

parameters.name: Slew
parameters.description: Sets the amount of slew applied to the scrub position.

parameters.name: Min CV
parameters.description: Sets the CV voltage corresponding to scrubbing to the start of the sample.

parameters.name: Max CV
parameters.description: Sets the CV voltage corresponding to scrubbing to the end of the sample.

input_ports.name: CV input
input_ports.description: Used to scrub the audio playback position

input_ports.name: Trigger
input_ports.description: Used to trigger the sample before playback or to change the sample

input_ports.name: Scrub input
input_ports.description: The bus to use as the scrub CV input.
